devTools.notDevDescription: This panel is only available when the server runs with MINERVA_DEV_MODE=true.
devTools.reseedDescription: Wipes the previously-seeded users, courses, documents, conversations, and messages, then re-inserts them from scratch. Hand-created rows are not touched.
devTools.destructiveBody: any rows tagged by a previous seed run are deleted, including their on-disk document bytes and Qdrant collections.
devTools.fixtureUsersDescription: The seeder always creates exactly this cast. Switch into any of them via the dev user switcher in the header (paste the eppn).
devTools.fixtureUsersTip: The calling admin (you) is not in this list; you stay logged in as yourself, and the fixture courses key off your id for ownership / membership.
devTools.fixtureCoursesDescription: Four courses spanning the configuration matrix you most often need to switch between.

report.embeddingNote: Documents are queued for embedding via the local fastembed provider; they will become searchable in chat over the next ~10-30s.

integrationKeys.createDescription: Issue a site-level bearer token for the Moodle / Canvas plugin. The plugin uses it to provision per-course API keys on behalf of teachers; it cannot access course data itself. One key per LMS instance is usually enough.

form.name: Name
form.domainsHelp: Optional. If set, the key can only act on behalf of users whose eppn ends with one of these domains. Leave empty to allow any eppn.

callout.title: Key minted for {{name}}
callout.note: Copy this now; Minerva only stores the hash. Paste it into the Moodle plugin's site settings.

integrationKeys.listDescription: Revoking a key invalidates it immediately. Any api_keys the plugin already provisioned via this site key continue to work (they're ordinary course keys). Edit a key's eppn scope inline to tighten an over-broad allowlist; the change takes effect on the next provisioning call without rotating the key.
integrationKeys.empty: No site integration keys minted yet.

editScope.label: Allowed eppn domains
editScope.emptyConfirm: Clearing the eppn-domain list means this key can act on behalf of ANY eppn (including admin accounts). Are you sure?

ltiPlatforms.setupDescription: Register Minerva as a site-wide LTI 1.3 tool. Any course on the LMS can then use it without per-course setup.
ltiPlatforms.dynregDescription: Paste this URL into Moodle's 'configure a tool by URL' input. Moodle will auto-install Minerva with the correct privacy settings, NRPS scope, claims, and custom parameters. No manual field copying needed.
ltiPlatforms.dynregMoodlePath: In Moodle: Site administration > Plugins > Activity modules > External tool > Manage tools.
ltiPlatforms.manualSetupHint: Only needed if your LMS doesn't support LTI Dynamic Registration, or to inspect the values dynreg would have set. Follow the steps below; the values at the bottom are quick-copy fields for each step.
ltiPlatforms.siteLevelNote: On first launch from a Moodle course, the launching teacher picks which Minerva course to link to. That choice is saved as a binding and reused for every subsequent launch from the same Moodle course.
ltiPlatforms.listDescription: Each entry corresponds to one Moodle/Canvas instance. Removing a platform cascades to its course bindings; teachers will have to re-link on next launch.
ltiPlatforms.copyValuesHint: Paste the Platform ID (issuer), Client ID and Deployment ID that Moodle/Canvas shows after you save the tool config.
ltiPlatforms.deploymentIdPlaceholder: Leave blank to skip deployment ID check
ltiPlatforms.domainsHelp: Optional. If set, only launches whose claimed eppn ends with one of these domains are accepted. Leave empty to allow any eppn (the legacy behaviour).
ltiPlatforms.approveScopeHint: Comma-separated list of domain suffixes the platform may launch users for (e.g. 'dsv.su.se, su.se'). Pre-filled with what the LMS admin suggested in the dynreg popup, if anything. Leave blank ONLY if you intentionally want to trust every claimed eppn (unsafe).
ltiPlatforms.approveEmptyConfirm: Approving with an empty eppn-domain list means this platform can mint launches claiming ANY eppn (including admin accounts). Are you sure?
ltiPlatforms.editScopeHint: Comma-separated list of domain suffixes this platform may launch users for (e.g. 'dsv.su.se, su.se'). Takes effect on the next launch. Leave blank ONLY if you intentionally want to trust every claimed eppn (unsafe).
ltiPlatforms.editScopeEmptyConfirm: Clearing the eppn-domain list means this platform can mint launches claiming ANY eppn (including admin accounts). Are you sure?
ltiPlatforms.pendingExplain: Installed via LTI Dynamic Registration. Until you approve it, this platform CANNOT authenticate launches against Minerva. Unapproved platforms are automatically deleted after 7 days. Verify the issuer and client id below match the LMS you expect, then approve.
ltiPlatforms.orphanedExplainPrefix: The LMS has been rejecting our client credentials since
ltiPlatforms.orphanedExplainSuffix: Likely cause: the tool was deleted on their side. Minerva will cascade-delete this row 30 days after that timestamp; remove it manually if you want it gone now.
ltiPlatforms.noBindings: No Moodle/Canvas courses have been linked to this platform yet.
ltiPlatforms.bindingsHint: Each row is a Moodle/Canvas course that has been linked to a Minerva course on first launch.
ltiPlatforms.nrpsHint: Names and Role Provisioning roster sync status for each linked course. New members are added and members who leave the LMS course are removed automatically.

ltiDiagnostics.overscopedLede: A per-course LTI registration on the Minerva side is receiving launches from {{count}} different LMS courses. Every launch funnels into the same Minerva course regardless of which LMS course the user came from. Almost always means the LMS-side install is site-level but the Minerva-side was set up per-course.
ltiDiagnostics.overscopedFixBody: Delete this per-course registration and re-register the tool as a site-level platform (Site administration > LTI). Or split it into one per-course registration per LMS context.

ltiApprovePlatform.title: Approve LTI platform
ltiApprovePlatform.description: Verify the issuer and client id match the LMS you expected, set the eppn-domain trust scope, then approve. The platform cannot authenticate launches until you do.
ltiApprovePlatform.alreadyActiveBody: Someone else already approved this platform. No action needed; the rest of the platforms list is back at the link below.
ltiApprovePlatform.notFoundBody: This platform doesn't exist, was deleted, or you don't have permission to see it. It may have been auto-cleaned up after going 7 days unapproved.

ltiSetupScope.lede: One last step before this platform can authenticate launches.
ltiSetupScope.warnBody: which eppn domains should Minerva trust this platform to launch users for? Choose narrowly: if the LMS is compromised, it can mint launches claiming any eppn within scope.
ltiSetupScope.noPresets: No suggestions available for this issuer. Add domains in the field below.
ltiSetupScope.customHint: Comma-separated. Added on top of any boxes ticked above.
ltiSetupScope.emptyWarn: Submitting with nothing ticked and no additional domains means Minerva will trust ANY eppn this LMS claims (not recommended).
ltiSetupScope.afterNote: After this, a Minerva integrator must click Approve in Minerva to activate the platform. They can adjust the scope you set here before approving.
ltiSetupScope.doneBody: Thanks. A Minerva integrator must still approve this platform in the admin UI before it can authenticate launches.
ltiSetupScope.donePrompt: If you're also a Minerva integrator, open Minerva now to approve the platform. Otherwise, hand off to an integrator and close this window.

users.title: Users ({{total}})
users.description: Manage user roles, daily AI spending limits, and account status. Setting a role manually locks it from auto-promotion rules.

courses.title: Courses ({{total}})
courses.description: All courses on the platform. Click the settings link to open a course's configuration.
courses.migrateButtonTitle: Force-migrate this course onto a different embedding provider/model. Re-queues every document for re-embedding.
courses.migrateDialogTitle: Migrate "{{course}}" to a different embedding model
courses.migrateDialogDescription: Pick the new provider and (for local) model. Admins can target disabled catalog models too; this is the way to walk a course off a model you've just disabled. Confirming bumps the course's embedding_version, opens a fresh Qdrant collection, and re-queues every document. The previous collection stays on disk (orphaned) so the change is reversible.
courses.migrateWarning: Saving will bump the course's embedding_version and re-queue every document for re-embedding under the new model. Search results will be incomplete until the worker drains the queue (a course with hundreds of documents can take several minutes).
courses.featuresButtonTitle: Toggle which experimental / opt-in features are enabled for this course.
courses.featuresDialogDescription: Per-course feature flags for {{course}}. Changes apply immediately; flipping a flag off reverts the course to the global default for that flag.

featureFlagDescription.course_kg: Per-document kind classification, cross-document linker, knowledge-graph viewer, assignment-refusal addendum, and per-chunk adversarial filter. Off by default while we calibrate.
featureFlagDescription.extraction_guard: Pre-generation intent classifier + output-side solution detector + Socratic rewriter, plus multi-turn assignment-proximity tracking. Catches the 'student pasted assignment, asks for code' case and intercepts the response with a Socratic question. Independent of course_kg so admins can pilot separately.
featureFlagDescription.aegis: Right-side prompt-coaching panel. Every user turn is scored on five dimensions (clarity, context, constraints, reasoning demand, critical thinking) plus three short callouts (structural clarity, terminology, missing constraint). Non-blocking by design - the analyzer races the assistant reply and the student can ignore the feedback freely.
featureFlagDescription.concept_graph: Eureka-2 concept graph: per-document LLM extraction of domain concepts and directed relations, persisted with document-level provenance and cosine/structural/community dedup. Distinct from the course knowledge graph (which is at document granularity). When on, admins can run extraction and view the graph; toggling off hides the admin endpoints but preserves the persisted graph.

courses.mergeButtonTitle: Merge this course into another and archive it
courses.mergeDialogDescription: Move all of this course's documents, conversations, members, Daisy offerings and other data into the surviving course, then archive this one. Moved documents are re-embedded into the surviving course.
courses.mergeWarning: {{source}} will be archived and all of its content moved into {{survivor}}. This cannot be undone from the UI.
courses.mergeNoCandidates: There are no other active courses to merge into.

suggestions.title: Suggested merges ({{count}})
suggestions.description: Active courses in the same semester whose course codes share a core (ignoring section / campus markers like MAR-, -S or (AB)). Pick the course to keep; the others are merged into it and archived.
suggestions.confirmDescription: {{count}} course(s) will be archived and all of their content moved into {{survivor}}. This cannot be undone from the UI.

bulk.archiveConfirmBody: {{count}} active course(s) will be archived (soft delete). They are hidden from teachers and students but can be restored at any time.
bulk.restoreConfirmBody: {{count}} archived course(s) will be made active again.
bulk.editDescription: Tick a field to include it; unticked fields are left untouched on every selected course. Identity fields (name, description) are intentionally not bulk-editable.
bulk.archivedWarning: {{count}} selected course(s) are archived. Settings changes only apply to active courses; archived ones are reported as skipped. Restore them first to edit.
bulk.semesterInvalid: Use the format VT2026 or HT2026.
bulk.embeddingWarning: Changing the embedding provider/model re-queues every document on each affected course for re-embedding. On a large selection that is a lot of re-ingestion; search results stay incomplete until the worker drains the queue.
bulk.systemPromptPlaceholder: Custom system prompt applied to every selected course...
bulk.featureFlagsHelp: Leave on "No change" to keep each course's current setting. "Revert to default" removes the per-course override and falls back to the global default.

fields.toolUseHelp: Give the model a tool catalog during the research phase. The save is rejected per-course if the chosen model does not support tools.
fields.semesterLabelHelp: Stamp a semester on every selected course, e.g. VT2026. Daisy-managed courses are overwritten on the next sync.
fields.embeddingHelp: Force every selected course onto this embedding provider/model.
fields.systemPromptHelp: Replaces the system prompt on every selected course.

rules.title: Role Auto-Promotion Rules
rules.description: Each rule promotes a user to its target role when ALL conditions match the user's Shibboleth attributes at login. Rules never demote, never override admins (MINERVA_ADMINS env), and never touch users whose role was set manually in User Management (those users show a "locked" badge; click Unlock there to re-enable rule evaluation). Multi-valued attributes (affiliation, entitlement) use list-membership semantics for "contains"; substring matches are rejected.
rules.negatedNote: Negated operators (<0>not_contains</0>, <1>not_regex</1>) also require the attribute to be present on the user. This prevents external/non-Shib users (who have no affiliation / entitlement / etc. at all) from matching a "not contains X" rule by virtue of having nothing.
rules.empty: No rules yet. Create one above to start auto-promoting users.

card.noConditions: Add at least one condition for this rule to ever match.

condition.suggestionHint_one: {{count}} suggestion (values seen on at least {{minUsers}} different users). Free text still accepted.
condition.suggestionHint_other: {{count}} suggestions (values seen on at least {{minUsers}} different users). Free text still accepted.
condition.suggestionEmpty: No known values for this attribute yet (need at least {{minUsers}} different users). Type any value; it'll start suggesting once more users log in.
condition.suggestionNoMatch: No suggestions match. Keep typing to add as free text.

externalInvites.createDescription: Generate a time-limited login link for someone without a Stockholm University account. Links bypass Shibboleth and grant student-level access; promote to teacher in User Management after they first log in. The full link is shown only once; if lost, revoke and re-mint.

callout.title: Invite link for {{eppn}}
callout.note: Share this link privately. It is shown only once and grants login until

chatModels.title: Chat models
chatModels.description: Catalog of chat / utility LLM models. A model can only be enabled once its provider key is configured and both USD prices are entered (0 is allowed for on-prem / free models). The 'Default' radio is the model new courses are created with; the 'Utility' radio is the model used for classification, knowledge-graph linking, and the price-scrape helper. Prices are USD per 1,000,000 tokens; spend is computed from token usage at these rates. 'Scrape' fetches the provider's public pricing page and asks the utility model to suggest rates for review; nothing is saved until you click Save.
chatModels.inputPriceLabel: Input price per million tokens for {{model}}
chatModels.outputPriceLabel: Output price per million tokens for {{model}}

storage.title: Storage
storage.descriptionTail: ; the host volume holds postgres, qdrant, documents and backups. Contact DSV helpdesk to expand when free space runs low.
storage.nearlyFull: Disk is nearly full. Request expansion from helpdesk soon.
storage.fillingUp: Disk is filling up. Consider requesting expansion.
storage.statsUnavailable: Disk stats unavailable on this platform.

system.documentsExternalHeader: Awaiting external sources (not a backend backlog)

classifications.title: Document classifications
classifications.description: Per-document kinds drive what the chat path will and won't paste into the prompt context. Sample solutions are excluded entirely; assignment briefs trigger a refusal addendum. Newly uploaded docs are classified automatically; this card lets you backfill any docs that landed before the classifier was wired in (or that errored on first try).
classifications.backfillTitle: Run the classifier on every unclassified, unlocked, ready document. Each click queues up to 5,000 docs.
classifications.lastQueued_one: Queued {{count}} doc; status updates as it runs.
classifications.lastQueued_other: Queued {{count}} docs; status updates as it runs.
classifications.note: Each click queues up to 5,000 docs. For larger backlogs, click again once the count has come down.

embeddingModels.title: Embedding models
embeddingModels.description: Catalog of local embedding models the runtime can load. Toggle 'Enabled' to control which ones show up in the per-course teacher dropdown. The 'Default' radio picks which model new courses are created with; only enabled models can be made default, and the default itself can't be disabled (clear it from another row first). Existing courses on a model keep working when you disable it, but teachers can't pick it for a new rotation. Throughput numbers come from running each model against a small academic corpus on this server. The 'warm' models are benchmarked automatically at boot (loading every catalog entry would OOM-kill the pod); the rest are benchmarked on demand. The server serializes runs, so only one model is loaded at a time.
embeddingModels.defaultAriaLabel: Make {{model}} the default for new courses
embeddingModels.runBenchmarkTitle: Load this model and run the benchmark corpus through it. Disabled while another model is benchmarking.
embeddingModels.runningHint: Currently benchmarking {{model}}. The page polls every 1.5s while a run is in flight and will update the row when it finishes.
embeddingModels.note: First run for a model includes a one-time download from HuggingFace, which can take a minute or two for the larger candidates. Subsequent runs are warm.
embeddingModels.confirmDisableTitle_one: Disable a model that {{count}} course is using?
embeddingModels.confirmDisableTitle_other: Disable a model that {{count}} courses are using?
embeddingModels.confirmDisableBody_one: The model "{{model}}" is currently selected by {{count}} active course. Disabling it removes it from the teacher picker but leaves the course on it; it keeps working under the model. To move it off, force-migrate the course from the Course Management page (admins can target any catalog model, including disabled ones).
embeddingModels.confirmDisableBody_other: The model "{{model}}" is currently selected by {{count}} active courses. Disabling it removes it from the teacher picker but leaves those courses on it; they keep working under the model. To move them off, force-migrate each course from the Course Management page (admins can target any catalog model, including disabled ones).

rerankerModels.title: Re-ranker models
rerankerModels.description: Catalog of cross-encoder re-rankers the runtime can load. Every RAG retrieval re-ranks its embedding candidate pool before the top chunks reach the model; this controls which re-rankers teachers may pick per course and which one new courses default to. Multilingual models cover Swedish course content. Unlike embeddings, switching a course's re-ranker has no re-embedding cost (the cross-encoder reads chunk text, not vectors), so disabling a model here is cheap to recover from. Only enabled models can be the default, and the default itself can't be disabled (clear it from another row first).
rerankerModels.defaultAriaLabel: Make {{model}} the default for new courses
rerankerModels.runBenchmarkTitle: Load this re-ranker and score a fixed query/passage set through it to measure pairs/sec. Disabled while another benchmark is running.
rerankerModels.runningHint: Currently benchmarking {{model}}. The page polls every 1.5s while a run is in flight and updates the row when it finishes.
rerankerModels.note: First use of a model includes a one-time download from HuggingFace. The default multilingual model is the lightest; the heavier multilingual re-ranker uses noticeably more memory once a course selects it.
rerankerModels.confirmDisableTitle_one: Disable a re-ranker that {{count}} course is using?
rerankerModels.confirmDisableTitle_other: Disable a re-ranker that {{count}} courses are using?
rerankerModels.confirmDisableBody_one: The re-ranker "{{model}}" is currently selected by {{count}} active course. Disabling it removes it from the teacher picker but leaves the course on it; it keeps working. The teacher can switch to another model with no re-embedding cost.
rerankerModels.confirmDisableBody_other: The re-ranker "{{model}}" is currently selected by {{count}} active courses. Disabling it removes it from the teacher picker but leaves those courses on it; they keep working. Teachers can switch to another model with no re-embedding cost.

defaults.title: System defaults
defaults.description: Platform-wide values applied to new courses and to runtime policy. Course AI defaults are snapshotted onto each new course at creation time; editing them only changes future courses (existing courses keep their per-course overrides). Platform knobs are read live; admin edits take effect on the next request.
defaults.resetTooltip: Drop the database row for this key. The next read falls back to the env var (if set on next restart) or the hard-coded value shown next to the field.

model.label: Default chat model
model.description: Chosen for newly created courses. Teachers can switch to any Cerebras-listed model on the per-course config page.

temperature.label: Default temperature
temperature.description: Sampling temperature applied to new courses. 0.0 = greedy, 1.0 = high variance.

contextRatio.label: Default context ratio
contextRatio.description: Fraction of the model's context window reserved for retrieved chunks vs. the user's conversation. 0.7 = 70% chunks.

maxChunks.label: Default max retrieved chunks
maxChunks.description: Upper bound on chunks pulled per retrieval pass.

minScore.label: Default min retrieval score
minScore.description: Absolute similarity threshold. Chunks scoring below are dropped from the context. 0 keeps everything.

strategy.label: Default retrieval strategy
strategy.description: `simple` = one parallel retrieval pass. `flare` = low-confidence detector iterates until coverage clears; requires a model that returns logprobs.

toolUse.label: Tool use enabled by default
toolUse.description: When on, new courses run a tool-driven research phase before the final answer. Requires a tool-capable model.

embeddingProvider.label: Default embedding provider
embeddingProvider.description: `local` runs FastEmbed on the pod; `openai` calls the OpenAI embeddings API (requires OPENAI_API_KEY).

systemPrompt.label: Default system prompt
systemPrompt.description: Prepended to every chat in a new course. Leave blank to use the built-in default prompt.

dailyCostLimitUsd.label: Default per-student daily spend cap (USD)
dailyCostLimitUsd.description: Applied per new course. 0 = unlimited (the per-owner cap is then the only spend backstop).

ownerDailyCostLimitUsd.label: Default per-owner daily spend cap (USD)
ownerDailyCostLimitUsd.description: Applied at first login. Sums spend across every course the user owns. 0 = unlimited. Existing users keep their current cap; edit per-user under Users.

maxUploadBytes.label: Max upload size (single doc, bytes)
maxUploadBytes.description: Cap for one document upload across teacher/Canvas/Moodle paths. Can be dialed down without a restart; raising above the router ceiling (2 GB) requires a deploy.

maxMbzUploadBytes.label: Max .mbz upload size (whole-course backup, bytes)
maxMbzUploadBytes.description: Higher than the single-doc cap because whole-course Moodle backups bundle multiple files; admin can dial it down.

canvasAutoSyncIntervalHours.label: Canvas auto-sync interval (hours)
canvasAutoSyncIntervalHours.description: How often Canvas connections with auto_sync=true are re-synced. 0 disables the background loop.

ltiNrpsSyncIntervalHours.label: LTI NRPS roster sync interval (hours)
ltiNrpsSyncIntervalHours.description: How often LTI 1.3 NRPS-backed courses reconcile membership with the LMS. 0 disables it.

observationTtlDays.label: Rule attribute observation TTL (days)
observationTtlDays.description: GDPR retention floor for the (attribute, value) suggestions that feed the role-rule editor. Values not re-seen within this window are pruned.

daisyImports.autoApplyDescription: When enabled, the nightly Daisy sync writes straight into the live courses table without showing up on this page. Leave off until you've watched a few syncs land cleanly.
daisyImports.autoApplyOn: Auto-apply is ON; new offerings appear directly in courses.
daisyImports.autoApplyOff: Auto-apply is OFF; new offerings stage here for review.
daisyImports.tableDescription: Each row is one Daisy offering whose next apply would actually change something. "New" inserts a fresh course; an update lists the staff and metadata fields a re-apply would add or change. Offerings that already match what's stored are not shown.
daisyImports.emptyState: Nothing to review. Either the nightly sync hasn't run yet, or every Daisy offering already matches what's stored (a re-apply would change nothing), or auto-apply is on.
daisyImports.selectAllAria: Select all pending imports on this page
daisyImports.applyResult: Applied: {{created}} created, {{updated}} updated, {{members}} members added, {{aliases}} aliases registered.
